Party_Details.Name: Leong Hin Chuee

Facts.Text: Leong Hin Chuee signed an Employment Agreement with Transpacific Group
Leong Hin Chuee received a monthly salary of S$25,000
The Employment Agreement provided for bonuses, share rewards, and reimbursement of expenses under clauses 5.3, 5.4 and 5.5

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the plaintiff is entitled to bonuses and reimbursements under the Employment Agreement
Party_Details.Rule: An employee is entitled to contractual benefits if the conditions stipulated in the contract are met
Party_Details.Application: The conditions stipulated in the Employment Agreement were fulfilled, entitling the plaintiff to the contractual bonuses, share rewards, and reimbursements.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court dismissed the plaintiff's claim because the corporate defendants were not parties to the Employment Agreement and the conditions for the bonuses and share rewards were either not met or were subject to the sole discretion of the employer.

Party_Details.Name: Citra Group Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Citra Group Pte Ltd did not bear the word "Transpacific" in its name
Citra Group Pte Ltd was not incorporated when the Employment Agreement was signed on 1 August 2007

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the defendants are legally responsible for the plaintiff's claims under the Employment Agreement
Party_Details.Rule: A corporate entity is bound by a contract only if it is a party to the contract
Party_Details.Application: The company was not a party to the Employment Agreement and is therefore not legally liable for any claims or benefits made under it.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court held that the corporate defendants were not parties to the Employment Agreement and thus not liable for the claims.

Party_Details.Name: Citra Group Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Leong Hin Chuee removed documents belonging to the Four Counterclaimants without their prior approval

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a former employee breached confidentiality obligations and fiduciary duties by removing company documents without authorization
Party_Details.Rule: An employee is legally responsible for maintaining confidentiality if bound by a confidentiality agreement and fiduciary duties
Party_Details.Application: The former employee breached confidentiality obligations and fiduciary duties by removing company documents and files without prior approval.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court dismissed the counterclaims, finding that the removed documents were the plaintiff's own files and emails necessary for his dispute, and there was no evidence of a breach of confidentiality that caused loss to the company.

Party_Details.Name: Leong Hin Chuee

Facts.Text: Mr Leong downloaded files and emails from his work computer to a portable hard disk before leaving his employment
The downloaded documents were personal files and emails necessary for resolving his ongoing employment dispute

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a former employee breached confidentiality obligations and fiduciary duties by removing company documents without authorization
Party_Details.Rule: An employee is legally responsible for maintaining confidentiality if bound by a confidentiality agreement and fiduciary duties
Party_Details.Application: The downloaded documents were personal files necessary to resolve an ongoing employment dispute and did not constitute a breach of confidentiality or fiduciary duties.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court dismissed the counterclaims, finding that the removed documents were the plaintiff's own files and emails necessary for his dispute, and there was no evidence of a breach of confidentiality that caused loss to the company.
